sections.title: ICT Asset Criticality Mapping
sections.content: Under DORA, scoping must start with the ICT Asset Register. Every asset in scope must be mapped to a 'Critical or Important Function' (CIF). If an asset supports a CIF, it is subject to more rigorous testing and reporting requirements.

sections.title: Third-Party ICT Risk
sections.content: DORA requires that testing includes third-party ICT service providers where they support critical or important functions. This scoping pack must include a list of involved third parties and confirmation of their authorization to test.

sections.title: Threat Intelligence Requirements
sections.content: For TLPT (Threat-Led Penetration Testing), the scope must be driven by current, relevant threat intelligence. The scenarios defined in this pack should match the TTPs of actors targeting the financial sector.

sections.title: Examination Readiness
sections.content: FFIEC scoping should prioritize areas identified in the last examination. Testing must be independent (performed by individuals who did not design or maintain the controls).

sections.title: Risk-Based Scoping
sections.content: Management must justify the exclusion of any significant business unit or technology from the annual testing program. This pack includes a justification section for exclusions.

sections.title: T1190 - Exploit Public-Facing Application
sections.content: Adversaries may attempt to take advantage of a weakness in an Internet-facing computer or program using software, data, or commands in order to cause unintended or unanticipated behavior.

sections.title: T1078 - Valid Accounts
sections.content: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts as a means of gaining Initial Access, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, or Defense Evasion.

sections.title: T1566 - Phishing
sections.content: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems. All forms of phishing are electronically delivered social engineering.

sections.title: T1059 - Command and Scripting Interpreter
sections.content: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands, scripts, or binaries.

sections.title: A01:2021-Broken Access Control
sections.content: Testing must validate that users cannot act outside of their intended permissions. Failures typically lead to unauthorized information disclosure, modification, or destruction of all data.

sections.title: A02:2021-Cryptographic Failures
sections.content: Testing must verify the protection of sensitive data in transit and at rest. This includes passwords, credit card numbers, health records, personal information, and business secrets.

sections.title: A03:2021-Injection
sections.content: Testing must validate that user-supplied data is not interpreted as commands or queries (e.g., SQL, NoSQL, OS command, ORM, LDAP, and EL expression or OGNL injection).

sections.title: A04:2021-Insecure Design
sections.content: Testing must evaluate the architectural design for missing or ineffective control design. Insecure design cannot be fixed by a perfect implementation.

sections.title: Control 1: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
sections.content: Actively manage (inventory, track, and correct) all enterprise assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments.

sections.title: Control 4: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
sections.content: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets and software.

sections.title: Control 12: Network Infrastructure Management
sections.content: Establish, implement, and actively manage network devices, in order to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerable network services and access points.

sections.title: CBEST Intelligence-Led Testing
sections.content: Testing must be driven by bespoke threat intelligence targeting the specific critical business services (CBS) of the UK financial institution.

sections.title: iCAST Execution
sections.content: Simulations must cover the 'Assume Breach' methodology to assess the detection and response capabilities of the institution's Security Operations Center (SOC).

sections.title: SR 13-1 / Vendor Risk
sections.content: The scoping must encompass critical third-party vendors and external facing infrastructure in alignment with FRB supervision expectations.
